method_atom: Building a non-Gaussian distribution as a continuous superposition (smearing) of Gaussian kernels indexed by a varying variance, and deriving the class of smearing measures over that variance for which the resulting family still satisfies the convolution/semigroup (Chapman-Kolmogorov) composition law rather than introducing memory into the underlying process. The same technique simplifies the associated hierarchy of moment-based evolution equations for conditional probabilities and reduces a full propagator computation to an easier averaged one over the mixing parameter.
object_atom: Heavy-tailed fluctuation distributions arising in quantum-mechanical processes and in financial-market price movements, whose propagators are treated via path integrals.
brief_reason: The variance-superposition of Gaussians, the semigroup-preserving smearing-measure derivation, and the induced simplification of moment equations and propagator integrals are the transferable machinery, while the quantum-process and financial-market fluctuation systems are the domain-specific objects.
